fields.description:
# Parallelize the container fan-out with warm-then-fan-out + fix the centrality budget cap

## Why
The container loop is sequential (`2*N` calls ≈ 27 min wall-clock). Parallelizing fixes latency. But caching
(S1) is write-once/read-many — a NAIVE concurrent fan-out makes all calls MISS+WRITE (≈20x input cost, no read
benefit). So parallelization must WARM the cache first. Separately, the per-plan budget cap scales UP with child
count (centrality), disabling shedding for exactly the large epics that need it.

## What
- Move the container pairings out of the sequential `for crit: for child:` loop into the existing
  `ThreadPoolExecutor` (`pass1.py`).
- WARM-THEN-FAN-OUT: run ONE warming call first, await its first streamed token (the cache becomes readable once
  the first response begins), THEN fan out the remaining pairings concurrently so they READ the cached prefix
  rather than all re-writing it. (Optional: a pre-warm call at gate start.)
- Fix the budget cap that scales with centrality (`sizing.py` `base*(1+centrality)`) so shedding engages for
  large epics instead of being disabled by the very signal (more children) that should trigger it.

## Scope
- In: container parallelization; the warm-then-fan-out sequencing; the budget-cap fix.
- Out: the caching mechanism (S1, dependency); merge (S4); bin-pack (S5).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Container pairings run concurrently in the thread pool (not the sequential loop).
- [ ] Warm-then-fan-out: one call warms the cache before the fan-out; on anthropic the fanned-out calls show
      `cache_read_input_tokens > 0` (NOT all writes) — proven by a test/measurement.
- [ ] The per-plan budget cap no longer disables shedding as child count grows (a large epic sheds as intended).
- [ ] An 8-child epic review wall-clock drops to minutes; `make lint` + tests pass.

## Remediation (opus review — MAJOR 4/5)
- The runner is NON-streaming (`run_sync` blocks). Warm = run ONE pairing TO COMPLETION, then fan out the rest —
  not "await first streamed token". The warm pairing is the FIRST REAL pairing; keep its findings; fan out the
  remaining N-1; aggregate must equal the sequential baseline (no dup/drop).
- There is NO open ThreadPoolExecutor at `_run_container` (the Pass-1 pool closed at pass1.py:336). Create a NEW
  pool inside `_run_container`.
- Gate warming on `est_tokens(plan) >= 4096` (cache floor; reuse det_floor.est_tokens) — else fan out directly
  (no warm), since a sub-floor prefix never caches and warming would just add a serialized call.
- Warm-call failure: systemic (LLMUnavailableError) -> abort like the Pass-1 tier (pass1.py:327-331); non-systemic
  -> degrade to a direct fan-out (accept all-miss), never hang.
- Budget cap: do NOT invert centrality — shedding marks G3/G4 INDETERMINATE, so the naive "fix" would DROP
  child-coverage/consistency on the most central epics (a fidelity regression the epic AC forbids). Instead:
  container criteria are NEVER shed; the cap bounds only overlay/agent spend; correct the COST MODEL
  (sizing.py:206 `len(cont)*n_children*COST_AGENT`) rather than the centrality scaling.
- [ ] Warm = first real pairing run to completion; aggregate finding set equals the sequential baseline (no dup/drop).
- [ ] A NEW thread pool runs the remaining pairings concurrently; on anthropic the fanned-out calls show cache_read>0.
- [ ] Warming is skipped when est_tokens(plan) < 4096 (fan out directly); warm-call systemic failure aborts,
      non-systemic failure degrades to direct fan-out.
- [ ] Container criteria are NEVER shed by the budget cap; the cap bounds only overlay/agent spend (numeric target stated).
